findings.message: The client now sends a query parameter `include_location=true` on the GET /inventory/{item_id} request, but the committed consumer pact (tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py) declares the interaction as `with_request("GET", "/inventory/1")` with no query parameters. The request the client actually makes now diverges from the committed consumer contract: the pact no longer represents the real request the consumer issues, so the published pact and provider verification will not exercise the `include_location` query the client depends on. Either add `query={"include_location": match.like("true")}` to the pact's with_request so the contract covers the new parameter (and regenerate/republish the pact), or drop the query param from the client.
findings.suggestion: Update tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py to declare the new query parameter in the pact interaction (e.g. `.with_request("GET", "/inventory/1", query={"include_location": "true"})`) and regenerate/publish pacts/demo-inventory.json so the consumer contract matches the request the client now makes.

findings.message: Provider-side change to the request shape (added include_location query param) means pacts/demo-inventory.json must be regenerated and republished; the existing committed pact and provider-verification job will not cover the new parameter until the consumer pact is updated.
findings.suggestion: Run the consumer pact test to regenerate pacts/demo-inventory.json and publish it before provider verification relies on it.
